## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName → FigJam file, use get_figjam

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	// Zustand state machine — OWNED by Dev C
2	// Read freely from anywhere. Writes only via the action methods below.
3	
4	import { create } from 'zustand';
5	import type {
6	  AppPhase,
7	  HouseId,
8	  CustomerProfile,
9	  DesignRefinements,
10	  DesignResult,
11	  RoofGeometry,
12	  AgentStep,
13	} from './types';
14	
15	// Custom address selected via Google Places Autocomplete on landing.
16	// Other devs (B = backend, A = scene, D = geometry) read this when
17	// `selectedHouse === 'custom'` to drive their own runtime pipeline.
18	export interface CustomAddress {
19	  formatted: string;          // e.g. "12 Lindenstraße, 14467 Brandenburg, Germany"
20	  lat: number;
21	  lng: number;
22	  placeId?: string;           // Google place_id, for caching/dedup if needed
23	  countryCode?: string;       // ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g. "DE")
24	}
25	
26	// Manual questionnaire inputs (only meaningful when selectedHouse === 'custom')
27	// Mirrors the Reonic onboarding flow exactly.
28	export type CalculateBy = 'usage' | 'cost';
29	export type Currency = 'EUR' | 'GBP' | 'USD';
30	export type HeatingFamily = 'renewable' | 'conventional';
31	export type PvPackage = 'starter' | 'comfort' | 'premium';
32	export type BatteryPackage = 'starter' | 'comfort' | 'premium';
33	export type ChargerPackage = 'comfort';
34	
35	export interface ManualInputs {
36	  // Energy consumption
37	  calculateBy: CalculateBy;
38	  consumptionKwh: number;        // residential consumption
39	  inhabitants: number;           // 1-4+ (people selector)
40	  electricityPrice: number;      // p/kWh or cents/kWh
41	  currency: Currency;
42	  annualIncreasePct: number;     // default 3
43	  timeOfUsePrices: boolean;
44	  // Existing solar
45	  hasSolar: boolean | null;
46	  // Heating
47	  heatingFamily: HeatingFamily | null;
48	  // EV
49	  hasEv: boolean | null;
50	  evModel?: string;              // free text e.g. "Elaris · Beo"
51	  evAnnualKm?: number;
52	  // EV Charger
53	  hasEvCharger: boolean | null;
54	  // PV package selection (Reonic offer step)
55	  selectedPackage: PvPackage | null;
56	  selectedBattery: BatteryPackage | null;
57	  selectedCharger: ChargerPackage | null;
58	  // Section completion flags (controls Save → collapsed summary)
59	  saved: {
60	    energy: boolean;
61	    solar: boolean;
62	    heating: boolean;
63	    ev: boolean;
64	    charger: boolean;
65	  };
66	}
67	
68	export type TrellisStatus = 'idle' | 'generating' | 'ready' | 'error';
69	
70	interface AppState {
71	  // Phase
72	  phase: AppPhase;
73	  setPhase: (phase: AppPhase) => void;
74	
75	  // Trellis (image-to-3D) generation status. Written by Orchestrator as it
76	  // walks the capture → clean → trellis chain; read by TrellisModel (which
77	  // shows the GLB once ready) and the design page overlays (which only
78	  // mount once the scene is interactive).
79	  trellisStatus: TrellisStatus;
80	  setTrellisStatus: (s: TrellisStatus) => void;
81	
82	  // URL of the generated GLB (set by Orchestrator when fal-ai/trellis
83	  // returns). TrellisModel watches this to load the mesh into the scene.
84	  glbUrl: string | null;
85	  setGlbUrl: (u: string | null) => void;
86	
87	  /** Set to `true` by <LoadedGlb/> after the GLTFLoader has finished
88	   *  loading the mesh. Means the GLB is parsed and mounted, but the
89	   *  skeleton→GLB cross-fade may still be running. */
90	  glbLoaded: boolean;
91	  setGlbLoaded: (v: boolean) => void;
92	
93	  /** Set to `true` by <MorphingBuilding/> after the skeleton→GLB cross-fade
94	   *  is COMPLETE and the GLB is fully visible at scale 1.0. This is the
95	   *  signal the panel-drop animation gates on — guarantees we never start
96	   *  laying panels onto a still-morphing or invisible roof. */
97	  glbStable: boolean;
98	  setGlbStable: (v: boolean) => void;
99	
100	  /** Rendered Y height of the GLB after the uniform XZ scale applied by
101	   *  <LoadedGlb/>. Used by <HouseGeometryProvider/> to rescale baked panel
102	   *  positions onto the actual roof — different houses have different roof
103	   *  pitches, so the analysis.json baseline doesn't match the GLB by default. */
104	  glbHeight: number | null;
105	  setGlbHeight: (v: number | null) => void;
106	
107	  /** Total number of panels actually fitted on the GLB roof by the variant
108	   *  cascade in <Panels/>. Published so the orchestrator's drop-animation
109	   *  loop knows when to stop (k-NN's target count can differ from this if
110	   *  the algorithm chose a compact variant for a tight roof). */
111	  panelTargetCount: number;
112	  setPanelTargetCount: (v: number) => void;
113	
114	  /** Total upward-facing roof area on the rendered GLB (sum of triangles
115	   *  whose world normal Y > 0.5), in square meters. Computed once after the
116	   *  GLB is in the scene so we can size panels relative to actual roof
117	   *  capacity and report coverage in the KPI sidebar. */
118	  glbRoofAreaM2: number | null;
119	  setGlbRoofAreaM2: (v: number | null) => void;
120	
121	  /** Rendered XZ bbox of the GLB (post-scale), in meters. Lets the panel
122	   *  packer know the world-space footprint to scan. */
123	  glbBboxXZ: { width: number; depth: number } | null;
124	  setGlbBboxXZ: (v: { width: number; depth: number } | null) => void;
125	
126	  // House selection — either a demo HouseId or 'custom' for an arbitrary address.
127	  selectedHouse: HouseId | 'custom' | null;
128	  selectHouse: (id: HouseId | 'custom') => void;
129	
130	  // Custom address (only meaningful when selectedHouse === 'custom')
131	  customAddress: CustomAddress | null;
132	  setCustomAddress: (a: CustomAddress | null) => void;
133	
134	  // Manual questionnaire inputs (custom address flow)
135	  manualInputs: ManualInputs;
136	  updateManualInput: <K extends keyof ManualInputs>(
137	    key: K,
138	    value: ManualInputs[K],
139	  ) => void;
140	  saveManualSection: (section: keyof ManualInputs['saved']) => void;
141	  resetManualInputs: () => void;
142	
143	  // Customer profile (auto-filled, then editable)
144	  profile: CustomerProfile | null;
145	  setProfile: (p: CustomerProfile) => void;
146	  updateProfileField: <K extends keyof CustomerProfile>(
147	    key: K,
148	    value: CustomerProfile[K],
149	  ) => void;
150	
151	  // Refinements (post-design)
152	  refinements: DesignRefinements;
153	  setRefinement: <K extends keyof DesignRefinements>(
154	    key: K,
155	    value: DesignRefinements[K],
156	  ) => void;
157	
158	  // Design result (from /api/design)
159	  design: DesignResult | null;
160	  setDesign: (d: DesignResult | null) => void;
161	
162	  /**
163	   * Number of panels currently revealed in the placement animation. Starts
164	   * at 0 once the imagery + sizing lanes have settled, then ticks up to
165	   * `design.modulePositions.length` over a few seconds. <Panels/> slices the
166	   * positions array by this count so only the first N panels render.
167	   */
168	  placedCount: number;
169	  setPlacedCount: (n: number) => void;
170	
171	  // Roof geometry — usually loaded from /baked/{houseId}-analysis.json by
172	  // HouseGeometryProvider. For custom addresses, /api/design synthesises one
173	  // and we stash it here so the Scene3D consumes it directly.
174	  customRoofGeometry: RoofGeometry | null;
175	  setCustomRoofGeometry: (g: RoofGeometry | null) => void;
176	
177	  // Agent sequence
178	  agentSteps: AgentStep[];
179	  setAgentSteps: (s: AgentStep[]) => void;
180	  updateStepStatus: (id: string, status: AgentStep['status']) => void;
181	  /** Patch any subset of fields on a single step (artifactUrl, resultLine, …). */
182	  updateStepFields: (id: string, fields: Partial<AgentStep>) => void;
183	
184	  // Reset
185	  reset: () => void;
186	}
187	
188	const defaultRefinements: DesignRefinements = {
189	  includeBattery: true,
190	  includeHeatPump: true,
191	  includeWallbox: false,
192	};
193	
194	const defaultManualInputs: ManualInputs = {
195	  calculateBy: 'usage',
196	  consumptionKwh: 3500,
197	  inhabitants: 3,
198	  electricityPrice: 35,
199	  currency: 'EUR',
200	  annualIncreasePct: 3,
201	  timeOfUsePrices: false,
202	  hasSolar: null,
203	  heatingFamily: null,
204	  hasEv: null,
205	  evModel: undefined,
206	  evAnnualKm: 15000,
207	  hasEvCharger: null,
208	  selectedPackage: null,
209	  selectedBattery: null,
210	  selectedCharger: null,
211	  saved: {
212	    energy: false,
213	    solar: false,
214	    heating: false,
215	    ev: false,
216	    charger: false,
217	  },
218	};
219	
220	export const useStore = create<AppState>((set) => ({
221	  phase: 'idle',
222	  setPhase: (phase) => set({ phase }),
223	
224	  trellisStatus: 'idle',
225	  setTrellisStatus: (trellisStatus) => set({ trellisStatus }),
226	
227	  glbUrl: null,
228	  setGlbUrl: (glbUrl) =>
229	    set({ glbUrl, glbLoaded: false, glbStable: false, glbHeight: null }),
230	
231	  glbLoaded: false,
232	  setGlbLoaded: (glbLoaded) => set({ glbLoaded }),
233	
234	  glbStable: false,
235	  setGlbStable: (glbStable) => set({ glbStable }),
236	
237	  glbHeight: null,
